food_name: Stir-Fried Beef with Vermicelli (Bun Bo Xao)
aliases: Beef and Glass Noodle Stir Fry

english: Beef and Glass Noodle Stir Fry

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Bun Bo Xao. FOODON_00002473 (beef dish) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (beef, stir-fried) is the closest parent; no specific code exists for the Vietnamese vermicelli preparation.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Bun Bo Xao or stir-fried beef vermicelli dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Bun Bo Xao is approximately 350 g, comprising a bed of rice vermicelli topped with stir-fried beef slices, vegetables, and aromatics.

nutrient_source.title: Beef, stir-fried with noodles — composite estimate based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked beef (ID 13397), rice vermicelli (ID 20113), and mixed stir-fry vegetables, weighted to a representative recipe proportion (40% beef, 35% vermicelli, 25% vegetables and sauce).
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Bun Bo Xao exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries: lean beef stir-fried (FDC 13397), cooked rice vermicelli (FDC 20113), and stir-fry mixed vegetables with soy/fish sauce seasoning. Recipe proportions: ~40 g beef, ~35 g vermicelli, ~25 g vegetables and sauce per 100 g dish. Sodium elevated to reflect fish sauce and soy sauce seasoning typical of Vietnamese stir-fry preparations.

gi_source.title: Rice vermicelli, cooked — GI value 53 (glucose reference); analogous estimate applied to Bun Bo Xao based on rice vermicelli as the dominant glycemic carbohydrate component.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bun Bo Xao. Rice vermicelli (cooked) has a published GI of 53 (low) per the University of Sydney GI Database. As rice vermicelli is the primary glycemic carbohydrate in this dish (~35% by weight), GI 53 is applied as the analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (53 × 57.75 g) / 100 ≈ 30.6. Available carbohydrate per 350 g serving = 16.5 × 3.5 = 57.75 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fish sauce and soy sauce
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size or request reduced-sauce preparation, as a standard 350 g serving provides approximately 1,470 mg sodium, representing over 60% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from rice vermicelli
type2_diabetes.guidance: The glycemic load per serving is approximately 30.6 (high GL category), driven by the rice vermicelli component; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider a smaller portion or substitute with lower-GI noodles such as konjac or whole-grain alternatives to moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from beef and elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish contains moderate saturated fat (~1.6 g per 100 g) from beef and elevated sodium from condiments; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size, choose lean beef cuts, and request reduced fish sauce and soy sauce to lower both saturated fat and sodium intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bun Bo Xao (Bún Bò Xào) is a Vietnamese stir-fried beef noodle dish consisting of thinly sliced beef wok-tossed with rice vermicelli (bun), lemongrass, garlic, chilli, bean sprouts, and spring onions, seasoned with fish sauce, soy sauce, and sesame oil. The dish is popular in Vietnam and is served at Vietnamese restaurants in Singapore, including at establishments such as Thien Long Vietnam Restaurant. The beef is typically marinated in lemongrass and fish sauce before being stir-fried over high heat, yielding tender, aromatic slices with a slightly caramelised exterior. The rice vermicelli provides a soft, chewy base that absorbs the savoury, umami-rich sauce. Nutritionally, the dish provides moderate protein (~10 g per 100 g) from beef, moderate carbohydrate (~16.5 g per 100 g) from vermicelli, and is relatively low in fat (~4.8 g per 100 g). Sodium content is elevated due to fish sauce and soy sauce seasoning, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic index is estimated at 53 (low) based on the rice vermicelli component, though the glycemic load per standard serving is moderate to high. The dish is dairy-free and suitable for those avoiding dairy, but contains fish (fish sauce) and soy allergens. It is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Bun Bo Xao exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional food composition database. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for the key components (beef, rice vermicelli, vegetables). GI is an analogous estimate based on the rice vermicelli component from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked lean beef (FDC 13397), cooked rice vermicelli (FDC 20113), and mixed stir-fry vegetables with soy/fish sauce seasoning. No direct food composition entry for Bun Bo Xao was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases at the time of processing. Sodium value (420 mg per 100 g) reflects the elevated seasoning typical of Vietnamese stir-fry dishes using fish sauce and soy sauce; actual restaurant values may vary. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. Halal status is marked false as fish sauce of uncertain halal certification is a standard ingredient; individual restaurants may use halal-certified fish sauce. GI of 53 is an analogous estimate based on cooked rice vermicelli per the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Bun Bo Xao was found. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (53 × 57.75) / 100 ≈ 30.6 for a 350 g serving. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from the USDA SR Legacy composite and should be treated as approximate. Vietnamese is the primary origin language for this dish; multilingual names for Singaporean serving context (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are also provided per G6 rules for Singapore cuisine context.
